item.title: Version 1.5.1
item.description:
What's New

  • Backlink context previews — related links panel now shows a snippet of context around each backlink
  • Backlink sorting — related links are now sorted for better discoverability

item.title: Version 1.5.0
item.description:
Inline AI Editing

  • ⌥J shortcut — opens an inline AI bar for AI-assisted editing directly in the editor
  • @-mention context — reference notes and folders in your prompt with @note syntax
  • Streaming responses — accept or reject AI edits, fully undoable
  • Draggable bar, multiline prompts, Retry — Shift+Enter for newlines; Retry replaces previous output cleanly
  • Anthropic API key — stored securely in the Keychain, configure in Settings

Performance

  • Eliminated per-keystroke view-tree re-renders
  • Cached gitignore scan with incremental single-file re-indexing
  • Skip redundant caret-move reveal work in preview mode

Fixes

  • GitHub PAT stored in Keychain and scrubbed from memory
  • Git failures surfaced to the UI

item.title: Version 1.4.2
item.description:
What's new

  • WikiLink editing fix — clicking a wikilink whose [[...]] markdown is exposed (in markdown mode, or in the active block while hide-markdown-while-typing is on) now places the cursor for editing instead of navigating. Links stay clickable in collapsed/inactive blocks.
  • Editor line-height fix — edit-mode line spacing now matches the rendered preview, following CSS line-height semantics instead of over-spacing lines.

item.title: Version 1.4.1
item.description:
Performance

  • Instant note switching — eliminated a ~1.5s stall on every note open and tab switch. The culprit was git log running synchronously through an interactive login shell, sourcing your full shell profile on every invocation. Local git operations now run directly (~0.02s vs ~1.8s).

item.title: Version 1.4.0
item.description:
What's new

  • Reveal raw markdown at cursor — when your cursor is inside a block (heading, callout, wikilink, code fence, etc.), the raw markdown for that block is revealed in place so you can see and edit exactly what you typed.
  • Correct font size for revealed blocks — revealed wikilinks and callouts now stay at the configured body font size rather than inheriting heading sizes.
  • Performance — debounced caret-move styling and cached preview regexes for smoother typing in large notes.

item.title: Version 1.3.3
item.description:
Bug fixes

  • Find & Replace: Fixed a crash when clicking Replace All in the note editor.

item.title: Version 1.3.2
item.description:
What's new

  • Find & Replace in the note editor. The Find bar (⌘F) now expands into a replace row with Replace, Replace & Find, and Replace All. Open it directly with ⌥⌘F or Edit → Find and Replace….
